meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Student Guide: Mastering Client Workflow to Ship AI Apps
content.seo_description: Stop building toy apps. Learn a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch real-world SaaS projects as a student developer.
content.answer_block: To break past basic tutorials, you must shift from 'coding' to 'engineering.' Adopt a professional client workflow by treating your project as a product. Use Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement. This process transforms fragmented code into a robust, deployable application ready for your portfolio.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks following YouTube tutorials, building to-do lists, and tweaking boilerplate UI. Yet, when you sit down to build your own idea, the screen remains blank. The gap between following instructions and architecting a product is where most student developers stall, trapped in the loop of 'tutorial hell.'

It is time to stop being a spectator and start being an architect. By professionalizing your client workflow, you bridge the distance between a raw idea and a production-ready application. We are moving beyond simple scripts to a professional development lifecycle that mimics the industry standards used by senior engineers at top-tier SaaS companies.

steps.title: Define the Product Scope
steps.description: Before writing code, define the core user journey. Avoid scope creep by limiting your MVP to one primary function.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Product Engineer. Review my app concept: [Insert Idea]. Create a functional specification document outlining the core user flow, data schema, and API requirements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Create a README.md outlining the project scope, tech stack, and MVP milestones.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Architecture
steps.description: Generate a robust project structure that separates concerns, making your codebase maintainable and scalable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern, production-ready directory structure for a Next.js and Supabase application. Include folders for components, hooks, services, and types.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Generate the project folder structure based on the architectural spec.'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Build features in isolation using a Test-Driven approach to ensure reliability before moving to the next module.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, modular component for [Feature Name] using Tailwind CSS. Include prop types and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Review the current codebase and implement the authentication module using Supabase auth.'

steps.title: Automated Code Review
steps.description: Use AI to audit your code for anti-patterns, performance bottlenecks, and security vulnerabilities before you commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a senior engineer code review on the following module. Identify security risks and suggest performance optimizations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Run a security audit on the current directory and suggest refactors for better performance.'

steps.title: Documentation & Handover
steps.description: Professional code is documented code. Prepare your project for deployment and future iterations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate technical documentation for this repository, including setup instructions, environment variable requirements, and a deployment guide.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Generate a comprehensive docs/DEVELOPMENT.md file based on the existing code.'

pro_tips: Always commit your changes to Git after every successful feature implementation to maintain a clean history.
Prioritize UI/UX consistency by building a shared design system file early in the project.
Use environment variables for all sensitive keys; never hardcode credentials in your repository.
Read the error logs first before asking AI for help; understanding the failure is the fastest way to learn.
Focus on building one 'killer feature' that works perfectly rather than five features that are buggy.
content.next_steps: Deploy your finished application to Vercel and share the link with your peers to gather real user feedback.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build beyond the basics. Stop waiting for the 'perfect' time to start; your portfolio is waiting. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
